scaled_oracle_cases.description: Happy path: TSV and YAML files with correct data are parsed successfully, preserving original behavior.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify baseline parsing still works after introducing new constraints.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must still correctly parse well-formed TSV and YAML files with recognized extensions.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A solution that breaks happy path would fail this test.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
tsv_path = 'exchange_limits.tsv'
with open(tsv_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('Glucose\t-10\t100\n')
    f.write('Lactate\t0\t50\n')
print('Parsing TSV file:')
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
for entry in parse_exchange_file(tsv_path, default_compartment='c'):
    print(entry)
yaml_path = 'exchange_limits.yaml'
with open(yaml_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('Glucose:-10:100:cytosol\n')
    f.write('O2:-1000:1000:mitochondria\n')
print('\nParsing YAML file:')
for entry in parse_exchange_file(yaml_path, default_compartment='c'):
    print(entry)
result = None
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the requirement for content-based format detection.
Scaled executable cases must expose the requirement for duplicate detection.
Scaled executable cases must expose the robustness requirements against malformed lines and mislabeled extensions.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: TSV file without recognized extension should be detected by content and parsed successfully.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that parse_exchange_file can detect TSV format by content when file extension is missing.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When file extension is not .tsv/.txt/.yaml/.yml, the function must attempt to detect format by reading the first line.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: The seed implementation would raise ParseError because resolve_format returns None.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the requirement for content-based format detection.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: TSV file with duplicate compound should raise ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that parse_exchange_file raises ParseError when a compound appears more than once.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must track seen compounds and raise ParseError on duplicates.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: The seed implementation yields all entries without checking duplicates, so it would succeed, not raise error.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the requirement for duplicate detection.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: TSV file with a line missing a field should raise ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that parse_exchange_file raises ParseError for malformed TSV lines.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Any TSV line that does not have exactly 3 tab-separated fields must raise ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: The seed implementation silently skips malformed lines, so it would not raise an error.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the robustness requirements against malformed lines and mislabeled extensions.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: YAML file with a line having only 2 colon-separated fields should raise ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that parse_exchange_file raises ParseError for malformed YAML lines.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Any YAML line that does not have at least 3 colon-separated fields must raise ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: The seed implementation silently skips malformed lines, so it would not raise an error.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the robustness requirements against malformed lines and mislabeled extensions.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: YAML content in a .txt file should be correctly parsed as YAML rather than TSV.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that parse_exchange_file uses content-based detection when extension is misleading.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: If file extension suggests one format but content is another, function must detect correctly based on content.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: The seed implementation uses extension only, so it would treat .txt as TSV and fail, or produce incorrect output.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the robustness requirements against malformed lines and mislabeled extensions.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: TSV file without recognized extension and duplicate compound should raise ParseError after content detection.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Combined test: content detection and duplicate detection must both work.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must detect TSV format by content, then detect duplicate compound and raise ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A solution that only handles one of the two requirements may either fail to detect format or fail to check duplicates.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the requirement for content-based format detection.
Scaled executable cases must expose the requirement for duplicate detection.
